response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with deep web, full-stack, and DLang experience, but is a poor fit for this role. C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no capital markets, investment banking, or fixed income domain experience, and no SQL Server/Oracle background — only MySQL/MongoDB. The gap on both mandatory technical and domain requirements is too large to justify an interview.
strengths: Solid engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing craftsmanship
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Broad polyglot background with strong communication skills evidenced by talks and workshops
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL-centric

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and performance-systems background
